additionalVolumeMounts.description:
[object] Extra volumeMounts for the Sealed Secrets Controller container
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/storage/volumes/

additionalVolumes.description:

http.description: Controller HTTP Port on the Host and Container

metrics.description:
Metrics HTTP Port on the Host and Container
Sealed Secrets controller ports to be exposed as hostPort
If hostNetwork is false, only the ports specified here will be exposed (or not if set to an empty string)

pullPolicy.description:
Sealed Secrets image pull policy
Specify a imagePullPolicy
Defaults to 'Always' if image tag is 'latest', else set to 'IfNotPresent'
ref: http://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/images/#pre-pulling-images

pullSecrets.description:
[array]  Sealed Secrets image pull secrets
Optionally specify an array of imagePullSecrets.
Secrets must be manually created in the namespace.
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/
e.g:
pullSecrets:
- myRegistryKeySecretName

port.description: Sealed Secret service Metrics HTTP port

ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/node-selection/

[object] Extra labels for Sealed Secret pods
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/

Number of old history to retain to allow rollback (If not set, default Kubernetes value is set to 10)

metrics.description: Metrics HTTP Port on the Host

apiVersion.description: Force Ingress API version (automatically detected if not set)

extraHosts.description:
[array] An array with additional hostname(s) to be covered with the ingress record
e.g:
extraHosts:
- name: sealed-secrets.local
path: /

hostname.description: Default host for the ingress record

secrets.description:
[array] Custom TLS certificates as secrets
NOTE: 'key' and 'certificate' are expected in PEM format
NOTE: 'name' should line up with a 'secretName' set further up
If it is not set and you're using cert-manager, this is unneeded, as it will create a secret for you with valid certificates
If it is not set and you're NOT using cert-manager either, self-signed certificates will be created valid for 365 days
It is also possible to create and manage the certificates outside of this helm chart
Please see README.md for more information
e.g:
secrets:
- name: sealed-secrets.local-tls
key: |-
...
certificate: |-
...

extraPaths.description:
[array] An array with additional arbitrary paths that may need to be added to the ingress under the main host
e.g:
extraPaths:
- path: /*
backend:
serviceName: ssl-redirect
servicePort: use-annotation

path.description: Default path for the ingress record

privateKeyLabels.description: Map of labels to be set on the sealing keypairs

ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#type-nodeport
NOTE: choose port between <30000-32767>

Custom startupProbe that overrides the default one

[array]  Sealed Secrets image pull secrets
Optionally specify an array of imagePullSecrets.
Secrets must be manually created in the namespace.

tag.description: Sealed Secrets image tag (immutable tags are recommended)

annotations.description:
[object] Additional annotations for the Ingress resource. To enable certificate autogeneration, place here your cert-manager annotations.
Use this parameter to set the required annotations for cert-manager, see
ref: https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/ingress/#supported-annotations
e.g:
annotations:
kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: cluster-issuer-name

apiVersion.description: Force Ingress API version (automatically detected if not set)

enabled.description: Enable ingress record generation for Sealed Secret

extraHosts.description:
[array] An array with additional hostname(s) to be covered with the ingress record
e.g:
extraHosts:
- name: sealed-secrets.local
path: /

extraPaths.description:
[array] An array with additional arbitrary paths that may need to be added to the ingress under the main host
e.g:
extraPaths:
- path: /*
backend:
serviceName: ssl-redirect
servicePort: use-annotation

extraTls.description:
[array] TLS configuration for additional hostname(s) to be covered with this ingress record
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/ingress/#tls
e.g:
extraTls:
- hosts:
- sealed-secrets.local
secretName: sealed-secrets.local-tls

hostname.description: Default host for the ingress record

ingressClassName.description:
IngressClass that will be be used to implement the Ingress
This is supported in Kubernetes 1.18+ and required if you have more than one IngressClass marked as the default for your cluster.
ref: https://kubernetes.io/blog/2020/04/02/improvements-to-the-ingress-api-in-kubernetes-1.18/

path.description: Default path for the ingress record

secrets.description:
[array] Custom TLS certificates as secrets
NOTE: 'key' and 'certificate' are expected in PEM format
NOTE: 'name' should line up with a 'secretName' set further up
If it is not set and you're using cert-manager, this is unneeded, as it will create a secret for you with valid certificates
If it is not set and you're NOT using cert-manager either, self-signed certificates will be created valid for 365 days
It is also possible to create and manage the certificates outside of this helm chart
Please see README.md for more information
e.g:
secrets:
- name: sealed-secrets.local-tls
key: |-
...
certificate: |-
...

selfSigned.description: Create a TLS secret for this ingress record using self-signed certificates generated by Helm

tls.description:
Enable TLS configuration for the host defined at `ingress.hostname` parameter
TLS certificates will be retrieved from a TLS secret with name: `{{- printf "%s-tls" .Values.ingress.hostname }}`
You can:
- Use the `ingress.secrets` parameter to create this TLS secret
- Relay on cert-manager to create it by setting the corresponding annotations
- Relay on Helm to create self-signed certificates by setting `ingress.selfSigned=true`

keycutofftime.description:
Specifies a date at which the controller should generate a new certificate. Useful in early key renewal scenarios.
Takes a date formated according to RFC1123. Can be obtained with the 'date -R' command on a unix system.
e.g
keycutofftime: "Mon, 14 Oct 2024 21:45:30 +0200"

keyrenewperiod.description:
Specifies key renewal period. Default 30 days
e.g
keyrenewperiod: "720h30m"
To disable use "0", with quotes!

keyttl.description:
Specifies the certificate validity duration. Default 10 years.
e.g for one year
keyttl: "8760h00m00s"

logInfoStdout.description: Specifies whether the Sealed Secrets controller will log info to stdout

privateKeyLabels.description: Map of labels to be set on the sealing keypairs

rateLimit.description: Number of allowed sustained request per second for verify endpoint

rateLimitBurst.description: Number of requests allowed to exceed the rate limit per second for verify endpoint

clusterRoleName.description: Specifies the name for the Cluster Role resource

clusterRole.description: Specifies whether the Cluster Role resource should be created

clusterRoleName.description: Specifies the name for the Cluster Role resource

create.description: Specifies whether RBAC resources should be created

labels.description: Extra labels to be added to RBAC resources

namespacedRoles.description: Specifies whether the namespaced Roles should be created (in each of the specified additionalNamespaces)

namespacedRolesName.description: Specifies the name for the namespaced Role resource

bind.description: Specifies whether to create a RoleBinding for the "proxier" role

create.description: Specifies whether to create the "proxier" role, to allow external users to access the SealedSecret API

subjects.description:
Specifies the RBAC subjects to grant the "proxier" role to, in the created RoleBinding
It is best to change this to something narrower, as the default binding gives `system:authenticated` access, which is very broad

revisionHistoryLimit.description:
Number of old history to retain to allow rollback (If not set, default Kubernetes value is set to 10)
e.g:

secretName.description: The name of an existing TLS secret containing the key used to encrypt secrets

annotations.description: [object] Additional custom annotations for Sealed Secret service

nodePort.description:
Node port for HTTP
Specify the nodePort value for the LoadBalancer and NodePort service types
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#type-nodeport
NOTE: choose port between <30000-32767>

annotations.description:
[object] Annotations for Sealed Secret service account
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/

create.description: Specifies whether a ServiceAccount should be created

labels.description: Extra labels to be added to the ServiceAccount

name.description:
The name of the ServiceAccount to use.
If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the sealed-secrets.fullname template

skipRecreate.description:
Specifies whether the Sealed Secrets controller should skip recreating removed secrets
Setting it to true allows to optionally restore backward compatibility in low priviledge
environments when old versions of the controller did not require watch permissions on secrets
for secret re-creation.

tolerations.description:
[array] Tolerations for Sealed Secret pods assignment
ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/

updateStatus.description: Specifies whether the Sealed Secrets controller should update the status subresource

watchForSecrets.description: Specifies whether the Sealed Secrets controller will watch for new secrets
